chunks.text: James Watson and Francis Crick published their discovery that DNA has the structure of a double helix.
But there was another scientist whose contribution was essential: Rosalind Franklin.
In her laboratory at King's College London
she was taking image of DNA by using the technique of X - ray crystallography.
One photograph, known as " Photo 51," clearly showed the double helix.
But her observation was shown to Watson and Crick without her permission.
The Nobel Prize was given in 1962 to Watson, Crick, and Wilkins.
By then, Franklin was no longer in this world.
She had died in 1958, at only thirty - seven.
The Nobel Prize cannot be awarded after death, by its rule.
Rosalind Franklin's dedication was forgotten for a long time.
Now, we must remember her quiet contribution.
The history of science is not made by loud voices alone.
